title: The Power Struggle at the Heart of Nepal’s Ruling Party
gist: Nepal's ruling Rastriya Swatantra Party (RSP), which won a near two-thirds majority in March 2026 on an anti-establishment wave, faces its most immediate threat from within: tension between newcomer Balendra Shah and founding chairman Rabi Lamichhane.

insights.claim: The most immediate threat to Nepal's ruling Rastriya Swatantra Party comes from within, centered on whether newcomer Balendra Shah, the former Kathmandu mayor who joined at the end of 2025, can work with founding chairman Rabi Lamichhane.
insights.speaker: Biswas Baral
insights.so_what: Intra-party rivalry threatens the stability of Nepal's new government despite its strong mandate.
insights.locator: The most immediate threat to Nepal's ruling

insights.claim: The RSP won nearly a two-thirds majority in Nepal's federal lower house in the March 2026 elections by riding a wave of antipathy against the old political parties.
insights.speaker: Biswas Baral
insights.so_what: A dominant mandate built on anti-establishment sentiment makes internal fracture, not opposition, the key risk to watch.
